sourceModel.description: Starter clinical dictionary for Find My Test autocomplete and concern association support. Concern grouping follows ICD-11 symptom/sign clinical-finding structure, with plain-language wording informed by trusted public symptom indexes.

disclaimer: Reference-support dictionary for triage-oriented test suggestion input only. Not a diagnostic engine.

symptoms.redFlagNote: Persistent fever with instability or confusion is urgent.
Rigors with hypotension or confusion can indicate severe infection.
Significant unexplained weight loss needs prompt evaluation.
Acute chest pain with dyspnea, syncope, or diaphoresis is urgent.
Acute or worsening dyspnea may indicate respiratory or cardiac emergency.
New orthopnea with edema can indicate decompensated heart failure.
Unexplained syncope may be high risk and needs urgent assessment.
Unilateral calf pain/swelling with dyspnea can indicate VTE.
Severe wheeze with exhaustion or hypoxia is urgent.
Pleuritic pain with dyspnea/hemoptysis needs urgent exclusion of PE.
Coughing blood is a red-flag presentation.
Severe abdominal pain with instability is urgent.
Persistent vomiting with dehydration or altered mental state is urgent.
New jaundice with confusion or bleeding requires urgent review.
Possible upper GI bleed is urgent.
Possible GI bleeding requires urgent review.
Significant or ongoing rectal bleeding is urgent.
Thunderclap headache is an emergency.
New focal weakness is emergency stroke-pathway territory.
Possible stroke sign requires emergency assessment.
Acute speech change is a red flag for stroke.
New seizure or prolonged seizure is urgent.
Acute confusion may indicate serious systemic or neurological illness.
Reduced consciousness is urgent.
Neck stiffness with fever/headache needs urgent assessment.
Visible blood in urine needs urgent evaluation.
Oliguria can indicate severe kidney or circulatory compromise.
Pregnancy with pelvic pain is a red flag.
Heavy bleeding or bleeding in early pregnancy is urgent.
Headache with visual symptoms or high BP in pregnancy is urgent.
Reduced fetal movement is urgent obstetric assessment.
Poor feeding in a neonate is a red flag.
Lethargic neonate requires urgent assessment.
Early or severe neonatal jaundice needs urgent review.
Unwell febrile child with reduced responsiveness is urgent.
Rash with fever and toxicity is urgent.
Persistent unexplained infant irritability may be serious.

signs.redFlagNote: High fever with instability is urgent.
Hypotension is a red-flag sign of potential shock.
Persistent tachycardia with symptoms requires urgent review.
Rebound tenderness requires urgent surgical evaluation.
Acute focal deficit is emergency stroke pathway.
Persistent post-ictal reduced consciousness is urgent.
Possible septic arthritis requires urgent assessment.
Non-blanching rash with illness is urgent.
Severe agitation with confusion can indicate acute illness.
Temperature instability in neonate is urgent.
Early or severe neonatal jaundice needs urgent review.
Poor perfusion in neonate is urgent.
Unresponsive or lethargic child is urgent.

concerns.redFlagNote: Urgent chest pain or ischemic symptoms require emergency escalation.
Severe dyspnea, hypoxia, or pulmonary edema requires urgent care.
Sudden dyspnea, pleuritic pain, hemoptysis, or unilateral leg swelling is urgent.
Hypoxia, respiratory distress, or sepsis features need urgent review.
Severe breathlessness or silent chest requires emergency escalation.
Possible sepsis is time-critical and requires urgent escalation.
Severe dehydration, persistent vomiting, or shock signs are urgent.
Acute jaundice with confusion/bleeding risk needs urgent assessment.
Potential GI hemorrhage is an emergency.
Severe abdominal pain with systemic upset needs urgent review.
New focal neurological deficit requires emergency stroke pathway.
Status epilepticus or reduced consciousness is urgent.
Suspected meningitis is a medical emergency.
Sudden severe headache or focal deficits require urgent evaluation.
Symptomatic hypoglycemia with altered consciousness is urgent.
Possible adrenal crisis is urgent.
Rapidly rising creatinine, oliguria, or hyperkalemia is urgent.
Active bleeding or severe thrombocytopenia requires urgent management.

associatedTests: ANA Screen and Reflex ENA Antibodies
keywords: ANA Screen and Reflex ENA Antibodies
relatedTests: ANA Screen and Reflex ENA Antibodies

concerns.term: Early pregnancy pain or bleeding risk
concerns.redFlagNote: Pregnancy bleeding with pain can be life-threatening.
keywords: Early pregnancy pain or bleeding risk

concerns.redFlagNote: Severe hypertension, headache, visual symptoms, or RUQ pain in pregnancy is urgent.
Rapidly spreading redness, fever, or systemic signs needs urgent review.
Any unwell neonate may deteriorate quickly and needs urgent escalation.
Very early jaundice, poor feeding, or lethargy requires urgent neonatal review.

concernModel.purpose: Grouped concern entries for autocomplete and future concern-to-test association logic. These are not diagnoses.
concernModel.backbone: ICD-11-style symptoms, signs, clinical findings, and body-system groupings, normalized into practical lab workflow categories.
